refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall establish and maintain a documented performance requirements baseline derived from worksite mission context, including cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability targets, with full traceability to worksite inputs and a managed history of all baseline and calibration updates.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct concerns: derivation (how targets are computed), recording (data capture), and management (lifecycle control). Refinement separates these into a coherent functional statement that emphasizes the baseline as a managed artifact with traceability and history. This aligns with the acceptance criterion requiring documented, traceable performance targets and baseline/calibration history.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (1) performance targets are derived from documented worksite mission inputs; (2) a baseline record exists with timestamp, source inputs, and derivation method; (3) historical records of all baseline updates and calibration adjustments are maintained with audit trail; (4) traceability links from each target back to originating worksite input are documented and queryable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context inputs.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record and store the performance requirements baseline with full metadata (timestamp, source inputs, derivation method, owner).
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed history of baseline versions and calibration updates with audit trail and change rationale.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Establish and maintain traceability links from each performance target to originating worksite input.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a centralized performance requirements database with derivation rules engine that computes targets from worksite mission parameters; store baseline snapshots with metadata; maintain version history with audit logging.
DP-B: Distribute performance target derivation across vehicle and teleoperation modules; each module maintains its own baseline records; synchronize via network messaging.

DP-C: Implement a performance requirements management service that ingests worksite mission context via API, applies derivation algorithms, publishes baseline updates via event stream, and maintains immutable audit log.
DP-D: Manual performance target definition by Site Manager via configuration file; store in local vehicle storage; track changes via file versioning.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Implement a performance requirements management service that ingests worksite mission context via API, applies derivation algorithms, publishes baseline updates via event stream, and maintains immutable audit log.

potential_sources: DP-C couples performance derivation logic to worksite mission input format; mitigation: define stable API contract for mission context ingestion.
DP-C couples baseline publication to downstream consumers (Vehicle, Teleoperation); mitigation: use event stream abstraction (Tasking interface) to decouple timing and format.
DP-C introduces dependency on network connectivity for baseline updates; mitigation: design offline fallback to cached baseline.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C is preferred over DP-B (distributed) because it centralizes derivation logic, reducing duplication and ensuring consistency. DP-C is preferred over DP-A (database-centric) because it emphasizes service-oriented architecture with event-driven updates, supporting real-time baseline propagation. DP-D is rejected due to lack of automation and audit trail. Coupling is manageable via API contracts and event abstraction.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., JSON, protobuf) at system initialization and during operation.
ASSUMPTION: Derivation algorithms for cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability are well-defined and can be expressed as deterministic rules.
RISK: If worksite mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derivation may produce invalid targets; mitigation: implement validation rules and fallback defaults.
RISK: If network link to performance requirements service is unavailable, baseline updates cannot be propagated; mitigation: cache last-known baseline and alert operators.
RISK: High safety criticality requires immutable audit trail; service must guarantee no loss or tampering of baseline history; mitigation: use append-only log and cryptographic integrity checks.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-C is sound. Recommend explicit design of: (1) worksite mission context API schema; (2) derivation algorithm specification document; (3) baseline update event schema; (4) audit log immutability and integrity guarantees.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Safety Services module shall own the performance requirements management service, including derivation logic, baseline storage, version history, audit logging, and event publication. Safety Services is the appropriate boundary because performance requirements (especially availability and safety-related targets) are critical to safety assurance and must be managed with high integrity and auditability.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services publishes baseline updates and performance target changes via Tasking interface to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules. Tasking carries the derived performance requirements as task parameters.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services receives worksite mission context and operational telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules via Telemetry interface to inform baseline derivation and calibration updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Safety Services uses Network Link to communicate with external worksite management systems for mission context ingestion and to synchronize baseline updates across distributed equipment.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level container but is too coarse-grained to own performance requirements management. Assigning to NGHE Solution would diffuse ownership and make accountability unclear. Not recommended.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing tasks and reporting telemetry, not for deriving and managing system-level performance requirements. Assigning to Vehicle would couple vehicle firmware to requirements management logic, violating separation of concerns. High diffusion risk.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operator interface and command relay, not for performance requirements derivation. Assigning to Teleoperation would introduce inappropriate coupling and diffuse focus. Not suitable.

Safety Services is the natural owner of performance requirements management because: (1) performance targets (especially availability, energy efficiency) are safety-critical; (2) audit trail and baseline history are essential for safety assurance; (3) Safety Services is responsible for safety-critical decision logic and data integrity; (4) centralized ownership ensures consistency and traceability. Excellent containment fit.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Safety Services is the appropriate module boundary for performance requirements management because it owns safety-critical data and logic, can enforce immutable audit trails, and is positioned to integrate worksite mission context with vehicle/teleoperation telemetry for derivation. The module has clear ownership accountability and minimal diffusion risk.
containment_risks: RISK: Safety Services may become a bottleneck if baseline derivation is computationally expensive or network-dependent; mitigation: design for asynchronous, event-driven updates with caching.
RISK: If Safety Services is unavailable, Vehicle and Teleoperation cannot receive baseline updates; mitigation: implement graceful degradation with cached baseline and operator alerts.
RISK: Tight coupling between Safety Services and worksite mission context format may reduce flexibility; mitigation: define stable, versioned API contract for mission context ingestion.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assignment to Safety Services is sound. Recommend: (1) define clear interface contracts for Tasking (baseline publication), Telemetry (mission context ingestion), and Network Link (external synchronization); (2) specify audit log immutability and integrity guarantees; (3) design offline fallback behavior for baseline caching.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (performance requirements management service with derivation, versioning, and audit logging) directly addresses all aspects of refined FR-005: derivation from worksite context, recording with metadata, management of baseline history, and traceability. The service-oriented approach supports the acceptance criterion for documented, traceable performance targets with managed baseline/calibration history. No decoupling or missing functionality detected.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK: If Safety Services is overloaded with other safety-critical logic, performance requirements management may be deprioritized; mitigation: establish clear module responsibilities and interface contracts.
DIFFUSION RISK: Network Link dependency introduces external coupling; if network is unavailable, baseline updates are delayed; mitigation: implement local caching and offline fallback.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Safety Services provides excellent containment for DP-C with minimal diffusion. The module owns safety-critical data and logic, can enforce audit trail integrity, and is positioned to integrate worksite mission context with vehicle/teleoperation telemetry. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, Network Link) are well-defined and leverage existing known interfaces. Ownership accountability is clear.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 is feasible via Safety Services module: (1) test derivation algorithms against known worksite mission inputs; (2) inspect baseline records for metadata completeness; (3) audit version history for all updates and changes; (4) trace performance targets back to source inputs via audit log. All verification activities are contained within Safety Services scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-005 to Safety Services is clear and unambiguous. Safety Services owns the performance requirements management service, including derivation, recording, versioning, and audit logging. Vehicle and Teleoperation modules consume baseline updates via Tasking interface and provide mission context via Telemetry interface. No allocation ambiguity or overlap detected.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to explicitly specify: (1) worksite mission context API schema and versioning; (2) derivation algorithm specification and validation rules; (3) baseline update event schema and publication frequency; (4) audit log immutability guarantees and integrity checks.
Define interface contracts for Tasking (baseline publication), Telemetry (mission context ingestion), and Network Link (external synchronization) to clarify data formats, timing, and error handling.
Specify offline fallback behavior: how Safety Services caches baseline, how Vehicle/Teleoperation operate with stale baseline, and how operators are alerted to baseline staleness.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 refined FR is not atomic; it comprises four distinct sub-FRs (derivation, recording, versioning, traceability) that require further decomposition. DP-C and Safety Services assignment are sound and pass all gates, but the sub-FRs must be decomposed to the next level to define detailed design parameters for each concern (e.g., derivation algorithm specification, baseline schema, audit log design, traceability mechanism). Proceeding to next level decomposition will clarify implementation details and reduce design risk.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context inputs.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record and store the performance requirements baseline with full metadata (timestamp, source inputs, derivation method, owner).
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed history of baseline versions and calibration updates with audit trail and change rationale.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Establish and maintain traceability links from each performance target to originating worksite input.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available in structured, machine-readable format at system initialization and during operation.
Derivation algorithms for cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability are well-defined and deterministic.
Safety Services module has sufficient computational and storage capacity to manage performance requirements service.
Network Link is available for baseline synchronization; offline fallback is acceptable with operator alerts.
Audit trail immutability and integrity are achievable via append-only log and cryptographic checks.
open_risks: Incomplete or ambiguous worksite mission context may produce invalid performance targets; requires validation rules and fallback defaults.
Network unavailability delays baseline updates; requires caching and offline fallback design.
Safety Services bottleneck if derivation is computationally expensive; requires asynchronous, event-driven architecture.
High safety criticality requires immutable audit trail; requires cryptographic integrity guarantees.
Tight coupling to worksite mission context format reduces flexibility; requires stable, versioned API contract.
evidence_needed: Worksite mission context schema and examples from representative worksites.
Derivation algorithm specifications for cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability targets.
Baseline record schema and metadata requirements.
Audit log design specification with immutability and integrity guarantees.
Traceability mechanism design (e.g., unique identifiers, link storage, query interface).
Network availability and latency characteristics for baseline synchronization.
Safety Services capacity analysis for performance requirements management service.
Offline fallback behavior specification and operator alert design.
